item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents mostly hold their opening positions. Agent A remains society-first by the end, with only a mild caveat that not every situation requires absolute protection of private rights. Agent B remains rights-first throughout. Probe movement is modestly convergent, but the text shows no clear reversal. Agent A's turn 3 appears to misread Agent B's position by saying it agrees with an argument that society should come before individual rights, even though Agent B argued the opposite.
